steps.title: Identify the dangling modifier problem
steps.content:
The phrase “Known as the Tunguska event, after a river that ran through the region,” is a participial phrase. Whatever subject immediately follows the comma must be the thing known as the Tunguska event—that thing is the explosion, not the forest, an “annual day,” or “approximately 800 square miles of forest.”
  • Choice A — the forest that inspired an annual day of asteroid awareness had approximately 800 square miles obliterated by the explosion:: makes “the forest” the subject—the forest was not known as the Tunguska event. ✗
  • Choice B — an annual day of asteroid awareness was inspired by the explosion, which obliterated approximately 800 square miles of forest:: makes “an annual day” the subject—the annual day was not known as the Tunguska event. ✗
  • Choice C — the explosion obliterated approximately 800 square miles of forest and inspired an annual day of asteroid awareness:: makes “the explosion” the subject—the explosion is what is known as the Tunguska event. ✓
  • Choice D — approximately 800 square miles of forest were obliterated by the explosion, which inspired an annual day of: makes “approximately 800 square miles of forest” the subject—the forest was not known as the Tunguska event. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The correct construction places “the explosion” immediately after the participial phrase, correctly identifying the subject the phrase modifies. That confirms C is correct.
